proposed_hypotheses.text: Overall objective_response rate in this 50,000-patient prostate cancer cohort is below 50% (i.e., a baseline minority of patients respond).

analyses.code: df['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Overall response rate is 0.2402 (12,011/50,000); the cohort is response-imbalanced with the no-response class dominating ~76%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary/integer features, at least one shows a large positive association with objective_response, suggesting it is the treatment indicator.
Several binary features show negative association with objective_response and behave like unfavorable prognostic markers.
feature_030 (constant zero) and feature_010 (5-level, 6-10) are not associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: for each int feature: chi-square pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_030 has only one value (constant 0) and is uninformative. feature_008 (binary) shows the strongest positive association (response 36.14% vs 15.89%, diff +20.24pp, p<<1e-300) — almost certainly the treatment indicator. feature_013 shows the strongest negative association (15.35% vs 34.60%, diff -19.25pp, p<<1e-300). feature_021 (15.0% vs 25.0%, diff -10.05pp, p=5e-56), feature_015 (16.0% vs 26.0%, diff -10.05pp, p=2e-98), feature_027 (17.6% vs 24.2%, diff -6.6pp, p=3e-9), and feature_001 (3-level: 28.2%/22.8%/18.2%, p=5e-72) are all negative/ordinal prognostic markers. feature_010 (Gleason-like 6-10) is not associated with objective_response (p=0.77). feature_006/005/023/011/017/019/004 are all null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 (long-tailed, possibly PSA) is negatively associated with objective_response — higher feature_022 corresponds to lower probability of response.
feature_020 and feature_024 are negatively associated with response; feature_002 is weakly positively associated.
feature_016 (age-like 30-90) is not associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_022'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_022'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_022 is 28.24 in responders vs 47.23 in non-responders (diff -18.99, t=-25.08, p=1.5e-137; point-biserial r=-0.087). Strong negative association — feature_022 is a major unfavorable prognostic marker.

analyses.code: t-tests for each continuous feature vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_020: 3.50 vs 3.95 (diff -0.45, p=7e-31). feature_024: 5.69 vs 6.23 (diff -0.53, p=9e-9). feature_002: 3.820 vs 3.796 (diff +0.024, p=4e-6, weak positive). All three reach significance.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on feature_016 by response status
analyses.result_summary: feature_016 (age-like): mean 65.01 (R) vs 64.98 (NR), diff +0.024, p=0.82. Not associated with objective_response. feature_018, feature_031, feature_026, feature_009, feature_029, feature_003, feature_012, feature_025, feature_028, feature_007, feature_014, feature_032 are also all null (p>0.1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 remains a strong positive predictor of objective_response (large positive odds-ratio) after multivariable adjustment for the other apparent prognostic factors, supporting its identity as the treatment indicator rather than a confounded marker.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[['feature_008','feature_013','feature_021','feature_015','feature_027','feature_001','log_f022','log_f024','feature_020','feature_002']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logistic: feature_008 coef=+1.2015 (OR=3.33, p<1e-300). feature_013 coef=-1.07 (OR=0.34), feature_021 coef=-0.74 (OR=0.48), feature_015 coef=-0.69 (OR=0.50), feature_027 coef=-0.43 (OR=0.65), feature_001 coef=-0.33 per level. feature_008 stays the dominant positive predictor — identifies it as the treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment (feature_008) raises the response rate by approximately 20 percentage points on average across the cohort.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_008')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Marginal response rate: feature_008=1: 36.14% (n=20,076); feature_008=0: 15.89% (n=29,924). Treatment-vs-control crude difference is +20.25 percentage points, chi-square p<<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 on objective_response is heterogeneous: a treatment×feature interaction screen will reveal one or more strong negative interactions, indicating subgroups in which the treatment is less effective.

analyses.code: for each feature x: sm.Logit(y, [1, t, x, t*x]); record interaction coef and p
analyses.result_summary: Interaction screen: feature_013 interaction coef=-1.978, p≈0 (largest). log(feature_022) coef=-0.568, p=1.5e-128. feature_015 coef=-1.237, p=1.4e-93. feature_021 coef=-1.138, p=3.3e-42. feature_027 coef=-1.283, p=1.8e-19. feature_001 coef=+0.141, p=1.6e-5 (modest positive). All other features show |interaction|<0.12 with p>0.02. Five strong heterogeneity drivers identified, all of which lower the treatment benefit when their unfavorable level is present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment (feature_008) raises objective_response sharply in feature_013==0 patients but provides essentially no benefit in feature_013==1 patients.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013 and compare treatment vs control response rates
analyses.result_summary: feature_013==0 (n=22,519): treated 60.96% vs untreated 16.93%, diff +44.03pp (p≈0). feature_013==1 (n=27,481): treated 15.81% vs untreated 15.05%, diff +0.77pp (p=0.088, n.s.). The treatment effect is qualitatively absent in feature_013==1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 is concentrated in feature_015==0 patients and is absent in feature_015==1 patients.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_015 and compare T vs C rates
analyses.result_summary: feature_015==0 (n=39,962): treated 41.05% vs untreated 15.95%, diff +25.10pp (p≈0). feature_015==1 (n=10,038): treated 16.50% vs untreated 15.65%, diff +0.85pp (p=0.27, n.s.). feature_015 acts as a treatment-resistance marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 is concentrated in feature_021==0 patients and absent in feature_021==1 patients.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_021
analyses.result_summary: feature_021==0 (n=45,004): treated 38.48% vs untreated 16.02%, diff +22.46pp (p≈0). feature_021==1 (n=4,996): treated 15.35% vs untreated 14.71%, diff +0.63pp (p=0.57, n.s.). feature_021==1 ablates the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Although feature_027 is rare (~3%), feature_027==1 patients receive no benefit from treatment whereas feature_027==0 patients do.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_027
analyses.result_summary: feature_027==0 (n=48,472): treated 36.73% vs untreated 15.81%, diff +20.92pp (p≈0). feature_027==1 (n=1,528): treated 16.23% vs untreated 18.44%, diff -2.21pp (p=0.30, n.s.). feature_027==1 fully suppresses (and slightly reverses) the treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment benefit declines with increasing feature_022; in the highest feature_022 quartile, the treatment effect is small.

analyses.code: quartile-stratified treatment effect on objective_response by feature_022
analyses.result_summary: Q1 (lowest feature_022, ≤6.46): treated 53.85% vs untreated 17.20%, diff +36.66pp. Q2 (6.46-15.86): +26.04pp. Q3 (15.86-41.28): +14.77pp. Q4 (>41.28): treated 17.32% vs untreated 13.89%, diff only +3.43pp (p=2e-7). Treatment benefit decays sharply across feature_022 quartiles.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level ordinal) modestly modifies the treatment effect — higher feature_001 levels show smaller absolute treatment benefit due to lower control response rates, but the benefit is still substantial in all three strata.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_001 levels 0/1/2
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=0 (n=17,592): treated 40.59% vs untreated 19.90%, diff +20.69pp. feature_001=1 (n=24,971): 34.79% vs 14.74%, diff +20.05pp. feature_001=2 (n=7,437): 30.08% vs 10.33%, diff +19.76pp. Absolute treatment effect is essentially constant; feature_001 is a strong prognostic factor that lowers both arms similarly. feature_001 is NOT a true treatment-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Restricting to patients with both feature_013==0 and feature_015==0 yields a larger treatment effect than feature_013==0 alone, because both markers independently degrade response.

analyses.code: df[(df['feature_013']==0)&(df['feature_015']==0)] then T vs C
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=0 & feature_015=0 (n=18,000): treated 71.67% vs untreated 17.08%, diff +54.59pp. Bigger than feature_013=0 alone (+44.03pp). Both markers independently necessary for full treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_021==0 to the favorable-marker set further increases the conditional treatment effect.

analyses.code: stratify by joint f013=0 & f015=0 & f021=0
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=0 & feature_015=0 & feature_021=0 (n=16,175): treated 78.00% vs untreated 17.33%, diff +60.67pp. Larger again. feature_021 adds independent information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete favorable subgroup feature_013==0 & feature_015==0 & feature_021==0 & feature_027==0 (~31% of cohort) is where essentially all of the treatment benefit lives; the treatment lifts response from ~17% to ~80% in this group.

analyses.code: all four favorable markers; T vs C response
analyses.result_summary: All-favorable subgroup (n=15,681; 31.4% of cohort): treated 79.78% vs untreated 17.21%, diff +62.57pp. Adding feature_027==0 yields a small but consistent boost. The treatment converts ~80% of patients to responders in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A logistic regression with a single binary 'all-favorable' indicator (fav4) and its interaction with treatment captures most of the treatment-effect heterogeneity, with a very large positive treatment×fav4 coefficient and a near-null main treatment effect outside fav4.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, [1, t, fav4, t*fav4])
analyses.result_summary: Treatment main coef +0.058 (OR≈1.06, p=0.055, near-null). fav4 main coef +0.141 (OR≈1.15). Interaction t*fav4 coef +2.885 (OR≈17.9, p≈0). Inside fav4=1, treatment OR ≈ exp(0.058+2.885) ≈ 19.0; outside fav4=1, treatment OR ≈ 1.06. The single binary fav4 captures the dominant heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the all-favorable subgroup, feature_022 no longer modifies the treatment effect — the apparent feature_022 interaction at the cohort level was confounded by feature_022's correlation with feature_013.

analyses.code: Within fav4=1 subset, sm.Logit(y, [1, t, log_f022_z, t*log_f022_z])
analyses.result_summary: Within all-favorable: t coef +2.948 (OR≈19.1, p<1e-300). log(feature_022) main coef -0.079 (small residual prognostic signal, p=0.005). t × log(feature_022) interaction coef -0.039, p=0.343 — NOT significant. By feature_022 quartiles within fav4: Q1 +63.3pp, Q2 +62.8pp, Q3 +62.4pp, Q4 +61.8pp, all very similar. The cohort-level feature_022 interaction was induced by feature_013 (which has feature_022 mean +1.38 log-units higher when feature_013=1, p≈0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the all-favorable subgroup, feature_001 (3-level prognostic) does not meaningfully modify the absolute treatment benefit — the absolute treatment effect remains 59-65 pp across all three feature_001 levels.

analyses.code: Within fav4=1, stratify by feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Within all-favorable: feature_001=0 (n=5,511) treated 85.86% vs untreated 21.31%, diff +64.55pp. feature_001=1 (n=7,824) 78.13% vs 15.94%, diff +62.19pp. feature_001=2 (n=2,346) 70.85% vs 11.88%, diff +58.97pp. All three differences are huge and similar; feature_001 mildly attenuates response in both arms but does not abolish treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the all-favorable subgroup, no remaining feature shows a strong residual treatment-effect interaction, indicating that fav4 captures essentially all of the treatment-effect heterogeneity.

analyses.code: Within fav4=1, run treatment×feature interaction logistic for each remaining feature
analyses.result_summary: Within fav4=1, top residual interaction p-values: feature_005 coef=-0.246, p=0.004; feature_017 coef=-0.285, p=0.033; feature_026 p=0.058; feature_001 p=0.069. None of these interactions yields a treatment-effect difference larger than ~5pp in absolute terms when stratified, and after Bonferroni adjustment for 26 tests, none survive (threshold 0.0019). No major residual heterogeneity remains.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four unfavorable markers feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 are mutually statistically independent (uncorrelated with one another), so each contributes additive subgroup-restriction information.

analyses.code: pairwise correlations and chi-square among the 4 markers
analyses.result_summary: All 6 pairwise correlations among feature_013/015/021/027 are between -0.004 and +0.000 with chi-square p>0.37. Markers are mutually independent. Marginal prevalences (==1): feature_013 55.0%, feature_015 20.1%, feature_021 10.0%, feature_027 3.1%. Combined fav4=1 prevalence is 31.4%, consistent with independence: (1-0.55)(1-0.20)(1-0.10)(1-0.03)=0.314.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013==1 is associated with substantially higher feature_022, partially explaining why feature_022 looked like a strong treatment-effect modifier in the cohort-level interaction screen.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on log_f022 by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: feature_013==1: mean log(feature_022)=3.55. feature_013==0: mean log(feature_022)=2.18. Diff +1.38 log-units (a roughly 4-fold geometric-mean increase), p≈0. feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 show only trivial associations with feature_022 (|diff|<0.04 log-units). The cohort-level feature_022×treatment interaction is largely confounded through feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner using random forests followed by a shallow decision tree on the estimated individual treatment effect will recover feature_013, feature_015, feature_021 as the dominant splits — confirming the binary-marker subgroup discovery without any model-driven prior.

analyses.code: T-learner: RandomForestClassifier on T=1 and T=0 separately; tree-fit on (predicted ITE > 0.3)
analyses.result_summary: Estimated ITE distribution is bimodal (median 0.11, P75 0.46) — consistent with a 'big-effect' minority. A depth-4 decision tree predicting ITE>0.3 splits first on feature_013, then feature_015, then feature_021, and at the leaves on continuous features (feature_024, feature_009) without changing the class assignment. The tree assigns class=1 (large ITE) only to feature_013==0 & feature_015==0 & feature_021==0; all other branches are class=0. Independent ML confirmation of the rule discovered by stratification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with at least one unfavorable marker (feature_013==1 OR feature_015==1 OR feature_021==1 OR feature_027==1), the treatment provides essentially no benefit on objective_response.

analyses.code: stratify by 'any unfavorable' indicator
analyses.result_summary: Any-unfavorable subgroup (n=34,319; 68.6% of cohort): treated 16.06% vs untreated 15.30%, diff +0.77pp (chi-square p=0.056). The treatment is essentially inactive in this majority of patients; their crude response rate (~15.5%) is the cohort-wide background response rate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all four unfavorable markers and their treatment interactions are entered jointly into a logistic regression, each treatment×marker interaction remains strongly negative and significant, while the marker main effects shrink toward zero — indicating these markers act as treatment-effect modifiers rather than as response predictors in untreated patients.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment main coef +2.624 (OR≈13.8, p≈0). Interaction terms: t×feature_013 coef -2.232 (p≈0), t×feature_015 coef -1.644 (p≈0), t×feature_021 coef -1.554 (p<1e-66), t×feature_027 coef -1.666 (p<1e-26). After interactions, marker main effects: feature_013 -0.035 (p=0.33), feature_015 -0.019 (p=0.63), feature_021 -0.099 (p=0.07), feature_027 +0.189 (p=0.028). The 'unfavorable' markers carry essentially zero prognostic weight in the untreated arm; their univariate negative association with objective_response is induced by the treatment-arm composition. They are pure treatment-effect modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final integrated subgroup hypothesis: in this prostate cancer cohort, treatment (feature_008) raises objective_response by ~62.6 percentage points (from ~17% to ~80%) ONLY in patients with feature_013==0 AND feature_015==0 AND feature_021==0 AND feature_027==0 (the 'all-four-favorable' subgroup, ~31% of patients). Outside this subgroup the treatment effect is essentially zero (~+0.8 pp). feature_022 (cohort-level interaction) and feature_001 (prognostic) are not independent treatment-effect modifiers once fav4 is conditioned on.

analyses.code: Compare T effect in fav4=1 vs fav4=0; report final OR/risk-difference
analyses.result_summary: fav4=1 subgroup (n=15,681): treatment risk-difference +62.57pp (17.21% → 79.78%); odds-ratio ≈ 18.9 (logistic). fav4=0 subgroup (n=34,319): treatment risk-difference +0.77pp (15.30% → 16.06%, p=0.056); odds-ratio ≈ 1.06. The treatment is functionally restricted to patients negative for all four markers feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 simultaneously. feature_001 attenuates absolute response in both arms equally and does not need to be included in the modifier definition.
